---
title: "[Project Title]"
subtitle: "Analysis – CIP Project Group [XXX]"
author: ["Andrin Kohler", "[Student 2]", "[Student 3]", "[Student 4]"]
title-block-banner: true
number-sections: true
number-depth: 3
toc: true
toc-location: right
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-tools: true
    code-line-numbers: true
    code-block-border-left: true
    css: style.css

execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
  cache: false
jupyter: python3
---

<!-- ============================================================
     CIP PROJECT TEMPLATE — Group [XXX]
     
     This template covers ALL mandatory requirements:
     ✅ Data Acquisition (Scraping + API)
     ✅ Dynamic element interaction (Selenium)
     ✅ Data Transformation / Cleansing (all 6 mandatory steps)
     ✅ Analysis & Visualization (3 RQs, min. 2 chart types)
     ✅ Results & Discussion
     ✅ Limitations & Outlook
     
     Delete this comment block before submission.
     ============================================================ -->

# Introduction

<!-- 
Short overview of the project scope and motivation.
What is the topic? Why is it interesting?
What data sources do we combine and why?
-->

TODO: Write introduction

# Data Acquisition

## Legal, Ethics, and Access

<!-- 
Document robots.txt compliance here.
Mention that no personal data is collected.
Reference the specific robots.txt rules you checked.
-->

TODO: Document robots.txt review and ethical considerations

## Environment Setup

```{python}
# Standard library
import os, re, sys, io, time, random, json

# Third-party libraries
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import plotly.express as px
from selenium import webdriver
from selenium.webdriver.chrome.service import Service
from selenium.webdriver.chrome.options import Options
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By
from selenium.webdriver.common.keys import Keys
from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import WebDriverWait
from selenium.webdriver.support import expected_conditions as EC

# Define base data directory
data_path = "data"
os.makedirs(data_path, exist_ok=True)

# File paths for data sources
# TODO: Define paths for each data source
# path_scraped_data = os.path.join(data_path, "scraped_data.json")
# path_api_data = os.path.join(data_path, "api_data.json")
# path_enrichment_data = os.path.join(data_path, "enrichment_data.json")
```

## Web Scraping with Dynamic Element Interaction

<!-- 
REQUIREMENT: "Find and interact with at least one dynamic element 
(search bar, buttons, list scroll, filter, ...) on the website."
REQUIREMENT: "Use Selenium and BeautifulSoup"

Document clearly which dynamic element you interact with.
-->

```{python}
#| eval: false

# --- Selenium setup ---
def create_driver():
    """Create a headless Chrome WebDriver."""
    options = Options()
    options.add_argument("--headless=new")
    options.add_argument("--no-sandbox")
    options.add_argument("--disable-dev-shm-usage")
    driver = webdriver.Chrome(options=options)
    return driver


# --- Dynamic interaction ---
# TODO: Implement interaction with at least one dynamic element
# Example pattern:
#
# driver = create_driver()
# driver.get("https://www.example.ch")
#
# # Interact with search bar (dynamic element!)
# search_box = WebDriverWait(driver, 10).until(
#     EC.presence_of_element_located((By.CSS_SELECTOR, "input[type='search']"))
# )
# search_box.send_keys("search term")
# search_box.send_keys(Keys.RETURN)
# time.sleep(random.uniform(2, 4))
#
# # Parse results with BeautifulSoup
# soup = BeautifulSoup(driver.page_source, "html.parser")
# results = soup.find_all("div", class_="result-item")
#
# driver.quit()
```

## Scraping Pipeline

<!-- 
Implement the full scraping loop here.
Include: caching/checkpointing, polite pauses, error handling.
-->

```{python}
#| eval: false

# TODO: Implement scraping pipeline
# Key elements:
# - Load existing data if available (caching)
# - Loop through pages/items
# - Polite pauses between requests: time.sleep(random.uniform(2, 5))
# - Save results as JSON after each batch
# - Error handling with try/except
```

## External Data via API

<!-- 
REQUIREMENT: "Ideally, you find data from various locations that have 
complementary character and provide the potential to create new insights 
through combining the different subsets."

Document the API, what data it provides, and how it complements the scraped data.
-->

```{python}
#| eval: false

# TODO: Implement API data retrieval
# Example pattern:
#
# api_url = "https://api.example.org/data"
# response = requests.get(api_url, timeout=60)
# response.raise_for_status()
# data = response.json()
# df_api = pd.json_normalize(data)
# df_api.to_json(path_api_data, orient="records", indent=2)
```

# Data Transformation / Cleansing

<!-- 
ALL 6 STEPS ARE MANDATORY (even if not all are directly meaningful):
1. Check for gaps / missing data
2. Check if columns show appropriate datatypes, change if needed
3. Check if values lie in the expected range
4. Identify outliers, treat them reasonably
5. Format dataset suitable for task (combine, merge, resample)
6. Enrich dataset with at least one column of helpful additional information
-->

## Load Datasets

```{python}
#| eval: false

# TODO: Load all datasets
# df_scraped = pd.read_json(path_scraped_data)
# df_api = pd.read_json(path_api_data)
```

## Step 1: Check for Gaps / Missing Data

```{python}
#| eval: false

# TODO: Analyze and handle missing values
# df_scraped.isnull().sum()
# df_scraped.info()
# Decision: drop or impute — document reasoning
```

## Step 2: Check and Fix Column Datatypes

```{python}
#| eval: false

# TODO: Verify and convert datatypes
# Example:
# df["date_column"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date_column"])
# df["numeric_column"] = pd.to_numeric(df["numeric_column"], errors="coerce")
# df["category_column"] = df["category_column"].astype("category")
```

## Step 3: Check Value Ranges

```{python}
#| eval: false

# TODO: Verify values are within expected ranges
# df.describe()
# Flag or remove implausible values with reasoning
```

## Step 4: Identify and Treat Outliers

```{python}
#| eval: false

# TODO: Identify outliers (e.g. IQR method, domain knowledge)
# Document threshold choices and reasoning
```

## Step 5: Merge and Format Datasets

<!-- 
REQUIREMENT: "Format your dataset suitable for your task (combine, merge, resample, …)"
This is where you join your scraped data with the API data.
-->

```{python}
#| eval: false

# TODO: Merge scraped data with API/enrichment data
# df_merged = pd.merge(df_scraped, df_api, how="left", on="join_key")
```

## Step 6: Enrich Dataset

<!-- 
REQUIREMENT: "Enrich your dataset with at least one column of helpful additional information"
-->

```{python}
#| eval: false

# TODO: Add derived or external columns
# Example: compute price_per_unit, add region names, categorize values
```

# Analysis and Visualization

<!-- 
REQUIREMENTS:
- Answer all 3 research questions
- At least 2 different visualization types
- Proper axis labels, units, captions, legends
-->

## Research Question 1: [Title]

<!-- State the question, show the analysis, visualize, interpret. -->

```{python}
#| label: fig-rq1
#| fig-cap: "[Caption for RQ1 figure]"
#| eval: false

# TODO: Analysis and visualization for RQ1
```

TODO: Interpretation of results

## Research Question 2: [Title]

```{python}
#| label: fig-rq2
#| fig-cap: "[Caption for RQ2 figure]"
#| eval: false

# TODO: Analysis and visualization for RQ2
# Use a DIFFERENT chart type than RQ1
```

TODO: Interpretation of results

## Research Question 3: [Title]

```{python}
#| label: fig-rq3
#| fig-cap: "[Caption for RQ3 figure]"
#| eval: false

# TODO: Analysis and visualization for RQ3
```

TODO: Interpretation of results

# Results and Discussion

<!-- 
This is the MAIN FOCUS according to the project description.
Summarize key findings across all RQs.
-->

TODO: Summarize and discuss findings

# Limitations and Future Work

<!-- 
Required: learnings, limitations, outlook on future improvements.
-->

TODO: Write limitations and outlook

# Appendix: Use of GenAI

<!-- Optional but recommended: document how AI tools were used. -->

TODO: Document GenAI usage if applicable
